content.full_text:
OPINION REQUEST MEETING PARTICIPANTS          May 10, 1978


                              Alan Herndon

                 Withdrawal of Statements of Organization




On April 28, 1978, I advised the Federation of Republican Women
that if any of their clubs had filed a Statement of Organization,
but had not qualified as a committee, they could withdraw their
Statements of Organization by filing a letter with the Secretary
of State's office stating that the clubs had never qualified as
a committee. This procedure was provided to clear the record
of these clubs because many of the 300 plus clubs in the state
had filed Statements of Organization, but had ceased filing
campaign statements.

The Secretary of State's office would like to see a form developed
for the purpose of withdrawing Statements of Organization.
What do you think?

Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Eu

111 Capitol Mall, Rm. 200
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

EPPC
POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-5662 12 31 PR'18

April 14, 1978


Mrs. Mildred Tucker, President
Council of Republican Women
ID Number 744457
743 B Avenida Majorca
Laguna Hills, CA 92653

Dear Mrs. Tucker:

Thank you for your letter dated April 8, 1978, regarding
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Your letter raises the question of whether your committee
has a campaign disclosure filing duty. Therefore, I have
forwarded your letter to the Fair Political Practices Com-
mission for response. The Commission has the primary
responsibility for administering, implementing, and explain-
ing the Political Reform Act. You may contact the Commission
at 1100 K Street, Sacramento, CA 95814, telephone number
(916) 322-5662.

If our office may provide any assistance to you in the future,
do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform Division


JANET E. SANDLIN, Auditor
Political Reform Division

cc: Fair Political Practices Commission

743 B Avenida Majorca
Laguna Hills, Calif.
April 8, 1978


March Fong Eu
Secretary of State
Sacramento, Calif. 92807

To the Political Reform Division:

Recently material was forwarded to me from Mrs.
Sara Evans, 111 Pent Circle, Anaheim, Calif. It was
an inquiry from your office concerning the "Committee
Status" of the Council of Republican Women - formally
the Orange County Council of Republican Women. Mrs.
Evans was a former Treasurer of the organization.

Our organization is a volunteer club. We do not
collect funds for candidates nor do we have headquarters
nor offices as we are not a campaign committee. In
reading the forms sent for the "Committee Statement"
we do not believe they apply for the reasons stated
above.

Past Treasurers have been sending "Short Term"
Statements to your office. How often should they be
sent or, being the type of organization ours is, do
you want those discontinued? What reports should
individual clubs send to your office and when? I very
much want to see that all necessary reporting is done
correctly so I shall appreciate any information you
can give me.

Thank you for your attention.

Yours truly,

[Signature]
Wildred Tucker
President
Council of Republican Women


Mrs. L. J. Tucker
743 B Avenida Majorca
Laguna Hills, CA 92653
